metadata.archetype_justification: Apache Druid is a read-heavy analytics database. Its primary surface is a query engine accepting SQL and native JSON queries, returning analytics results. Query execution dominates traffic (>90% reads), fitting the data-gateway archetype.
metadata.context: Apache Druid: high-performance real-time analytics database.

evaluations.trigger: Service has operations >30s OR long-running workflows
Service has persistent state (stateful-crud, data-gateway, orchestrator)
Service has external dependencies (calls other services or external APIs)
Service is P0 priority OR is on the critical path

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication for Agent Principals
findings.description: Druid's authentication framework is pluggable (Authenticator interface chain) and supports HTTP Basic, Kerberos, LDAP, and PAC4J/OIDC. However, there is no native support for service account or machine identity authentication with principal attribution for autonomous agents.
findings.gap: No OAuth2 client credentials flow, no API key with principal attribution, no mTLS-based service account mechanism designed for agent identities. Existing auth mechanisms target human users or inter-node communication.
findings.recommendation: Implement an Authenticator extension supporting OAuth2 client_credentials or API key-based machine identity with per-agent principal attribution. Alternatively, deploy an API Gateway that handles machine identity and passes authenticated principal headers.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Deploy an API Gateway (AWS API Gateway, Kong) in front of Druid that handles machine identity via OAuth2 client_credentials and passes authenticated principal headers.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Agent identities are individually authenticated, attributed in audit logs, and distinguishable from human users.

findings.title: Coarse-Grained Permission Scoping
findings.description: Druid's authorization model supports resource-type + regex scoping with READ/WRITE actions but operates at a coarse granularity without endpoint-level scoping for agent identities.
findings.gap: No fine-grained endpoint-level permission scoping. An agent granted READ on datasources gets READ on all matching datasources.
findings.recommendation: Create dedicated authorization roles for agent identities with minimal resource-regex patterns. Consider extending the Authorizer to support endpoint-level scoping.

compensating_controls: Deploy an API Gateway with fine-grained route-level policies restricting agent access to specific endpoints
Create dedicated Druid users with narrow resource-regex patterns limiting to specific datasources

findings.title: Audit Logs Not Tamper-Evident
findings.description: Druid has an AuditManager framework (SQL and Logging implementations). Audit logs are append-only but not immutable — removeAuditLogsOlderThan() allows deletion, and there is no cryptographic hash chaining or tamper-evidence mechanism.
findings.gap: Audit logs are not tamper-evident. Database administrators with metadata store access can modify or delete entries without detection. No CloudTrail integration.
findings.recommendation: Implement an AuditManager extension that writes to an immutable store (S3 with Object Lock) or integrate with AWS CloudTrail via an API Gateway proxy.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, downgraded from BLOCKER
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so immutable audit logging for writes is not a deployment blocker. However, audit logging for read access patterns is still a safety concern.
compensating_controls: Stream audit events to an immutable external store (S3 with Object Lock, CloudWatch Logs with retention lock)
Deploy a LoggingAuditManager that writes to a tamper-evident log aggregation system

findings.title: No Immediate Agent Identity Suspension
findings.description: User disablement is available via coordinator API but depends on authentication cache refresh interval. There is no instant propagation mechanism for agent identity lockout across all Druid nodes.
findings.gap: No immediate, propagated suspension mechanism. Cache-based auth means a non-zero window between disabling and actual lockout.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an API Gateway with instant API key revocation capability for agent identities, independent of Druid's internal auth cache refresh.

compensating_controls: Set BasicHTTPAuthenticator cache duration to minimum value for near-immediate propagation
Deploy an API Gateway with API key revocation capability for instant agent lockout

findings.title: No HTTP-Level Rate Limiting
findings.description: Druid has no HTTP-level rate limiting for REST APIs. Only internal query scheduling via QueryScheduler with resilience4j bulkheads for query lane isolation. No API Gateway, WAF, or application-level rate-limiting middleware.
findings.gap: Unlimited API requests possible from any client. No request-level rate limiting.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an API Gateway with usage plans and throttling in front of all Druid API endpoints. Configure per-agent rate limits.

compensating_controls: Deploy an API Gateway (AWS API Gateway, Kong, Envoy) with throttling policies
Configure query lane isolation to limit agent queries to a dedicated lane with bounded concurrency

findings.title: No PII Redaction in Logs
findings.description: Druid uses Log4j2 and emits query text, user identity, and request context in logs without PII scrubbing. The AuditManager stores full payloads without redaction.
findings.gap: No PII redaction in logs. User-submitted query text, identifiers, and configuration payloads logged verbatim.
findings.recommendation: Implement a custom Log4j2 filter that redacts PII patterns. For query logs, redact literal values from SQL/native query text before logging.

compensating_controls: Configure Log4j2 pattern layouts to exclude sensitive fields
Deploy a log aggregation layer with field-level masking

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, or machine-readable API specification exists. APIs are defined through JAX-RS annotations only.
findings.gap: No machine-readable spec for the full REST API. Agent tool generation requires manual work.
findings.recommendation: Integrate swagger-jaxrs2 to auto-generate OpenAPI specs from existing JAX-RS annotations.

compensating_controls: Use JAX-RS annotation scanning tools to auto-generate OpenAPI specs
Maintain a manually curated tool definition for the subset of APIs agents will use

findings.title: Inconsistent Structured Error Responses
findings.description: DruidException provides structured errors with error codes and HTTP status categories. However, not all endpoints use it consistently, and no retryable field exists.
findings.gap: Inconsistent error format across endpoints. No explicit retryability signal.
findings.recommendation: Add a retryable boolean field to error responses. Standardize all endpoints through DruidException.

compensating_controls: Document which HTTP status codes are retriable in agent tool definitions
Build agent-side logic to interpret DruidException error codes

findings.title: Management APIs Lack Pagination
findings.description: Query APIs support LIMIT/OFFSET. Management REST APIs (datasources, tasks, segments) have inconsistent pagination — many list endpoints return unbounded result sets.
findings.gap: Management API endpoints return unbounded collections.
findings.recommendation: Add pagination parameters (limit, offset/cursor) to management REST API endpoints.

compensating_controls: Agent tool definitions can enforce client-side result-size limits
Restrict management API calls to specific named resources

findings.title: No Automated API Breaking Change Detection
findings.description: Path-based versioning exists (/v1/, /v2/). No automated breaking change detection in CI, no contract testing, no deprecation mechanism.
findings.gap: No automated API contract stability validation in CI.
findings.recommendation: Integrate OpenAPI diff tooling into CI to detect breaking changes after spec generation.

compensating_controls: Pin agent tool definitions to specific Druid versions
Monitor Druid release notes for breaking API changes manually

findings.title: Shallow Distributed Tracing
findings.description: Contrib OpenTelemetry emitter covers query metrics only. No native distributed tracing across Druid service nodes. Text-based logging default. Query IDs exist but no request-level correlation propagated.
findings.gap: No full distributed tracing. No structured JSON logging by default.
findings.recommendation: Promote OpenTelemetry to core. Implement full request tracing with trace ID propagation. Configure structured JSON logging.

compensating_controls: Deploy the OpenTelemetry contrib extension and configure Log4j2 JSON layout
Use query IDs for manual request correlation in log aggregation

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration in Repository
findings.description: Metric emission framework exists via ServiceEmitter. No alerting thresholds or alarm configurations defined in the codebase.
findings.gap: No pre-defined alerting thresholds for agent-relevant error rates.
findings.recommendation: Provide reference alerting configurations (CloudWatch alarm templates or Prometheus recording rules).

compensating_controls: Configure alerting externally using Druid's emitted metrics
Define SLO-based alerts in deployment platform

findings.title: No Infrastructure-as-Code for Production Deployment
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize in the repository. Only a Docker Compose for local dev and a Dockerfile.
findings.gap: No IaC for production deployment, drift detection, or peer-reviewed infrastructure changes.
findings.recommendation: Provide reference IaC (Helm chart or Terraform module) for production deployment with security best practices.

compensating_controls: Operators deploy using external IaC (community Helm charts, Terraform modules)
Docker image provides a reproducible runtime base

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: Comprehensive CI/CD with 13 workflows. No API contract testing — no Pact, OpenAPI validation, or schema comparison.
findings.gap: No automated detection of API-breaking changes in CI.
findings.recommendation: Integrate OpenAPI diff tool into CI after generating specs.

compensating_controls: Extensive integration test suite catches behavioral regressions
Human PR review of API changes

findings.title: No Rollback Configuration
findings.description: No blue-green, canary, or rollback strategy defined in the repository. Rollback is entirely an operator responsibility.
findings.gap: No rollback mechanism defined in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Provide reference deployment configurations with rollback capabilities (Helm rollback, blue-green patterns).

compensating_controls: Operators implement rollback in their own deployment pipelines
Druid's rolling upgrade support enables node-by-node rollback

findings.title: No Staging Environment with Realistic Data
findings.description: Docker Compose provides local development only. No dedicated staging environment configuration with production-equivalent data shape or synthetic data generators.
findings.gap: Docker Compose is for development, not a staging environment with realistic data volumes.
findings.recommendation: Provide reference configurations for a staging environment with synthetic data generation scripts.

compensating_controls: Use Docker Compose with custom data ingestion scripts for testing
Operators maintain their own staging environments externally

findings.title: No Data Residency Enforcement
findings.description: Druid stores data in configurable deep storage (S3, HDFS, Azure, GCS). No built-in data residency enforcement — location depends on deployment configuration.
findings.gap: No data residency enforcement or awareness at the application layer. No region-tagging of data.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency requirements per datasource. Enforce at the deployment layer. Consider datasource-level metadata tags for regulatory classification.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, downgraded from BLOCKER
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents query data without modifying it, but may still transmit results to LLM endpoints in different regions. Risk profile is lower than write-enabled but residency compliance remains a safety concern.
compensating_controls: Deploy Druid and LLM endpoints in the same AWS region
Restrict agent queries to non-regulated datasources via authorization

findings.title: No HTTP-Level Idempotency Keys (Read-Only INFO)
findings.description: No HTTP-level idempotency keys on write endpoints. Internal operation-level idempotency exists. Read-only scope means this is informational.
findings.gap: No idempotency key support on write endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Plan idempotency if expanding to write-enabled scope.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so idempotency is informational only.

findings.title: Structured JSON Response Format
findings.description: All Druid API responses use structured JSON consistently.
findings.gap: None.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: No External Event Emission
findings.description: No webhooks or external event notification for state changes. Internal ServiceEmitter for metrics only.
findings.gap: No event-driven notification for agents.
findings.recommendation: Consider webhook/EventBridge integration for key state transitions.

findings.title: No Rate Limit Headers
findings.description: No rate limit headers returned. No rate limiting documentation.
findings.gap: No self-throttling signal for agents.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limit headers when rate limiting is implemented.

findings.title: No Identity Delegation (Data-Gateway INFO)
findings.description: Internal identity propagation via AuthenticationResult. No OAuth2 token exchange. Downgraded to INFO for data-gateway archetype.
findings.gap: No identity delegation for multi-tenant agent scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Revisit if multi-tenant agent scenarios arise.

findings.title: Environment-Variable Credential Management
findings.description: Extensible DynamicConfigProvider interface. Default is environment variables. No native Vault/Secrets Manager integration.
findings.gap: No integrated secrets management with rotation.
findings.recommendation: Implement DynamicConfigProvider for AWS Secrets Manager.

findings.title: No Multi-Step Rollback (Read-Only INFO)
findings.description: No explicit compensation or rollback for multi-step operations. Read-only scope and data-gateway archetype — queries are stateless.
findings.gap: No multi-step rollback.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND data-gateway archetype calibration
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents on a data-gateway do not execute multi-step write workflows. Queries are stateless operations.

findings.title: No API-Level Concurrency Controls (Read-Only INFO)
findings.description: Internal optimistic concurrency exists. No ETags on REST API. Read-only scope — not relevant.
findings.gap: No API-level concurrency controls exposed.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

findings.title: No Per-Agent Transaction Limits (Read-Only INFO)
findings.description: System-wide query limits exist. No per-agent configurable transaction limits. Read-only scope — informational.
findings.gap: No per-identity blast radius controls.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

findings.title: No Draft/Pending State (Read-Only INFO)
findings.description: No user-facing draft/pending state. Operations execute immediately. Read-only scope — not relevant.
findings.gap: No draft state for agent-proposed changes.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

findings.title: No Approval Gates (Read-Only INFO)
findings.description: No approval gate mechanism exists. Read-only scope — not relevant.
findings.gap: No human-in-the-loop gates.
findings.recommendation: No action for read-only scope.

findings.title: Data Classification Depends on Deployment (INFO)
findings.description: Druid stores whatever users ingest. Data sensitivity depends on deployment. Authorization scopes to datasource level. Data-gateway archetype calibration applies.
findings.gap: No field-level access control in query responses.
findings.recommendation: Restrict agent identities to non-sensitive datasources via resource authorization.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: data-gateway archetype calibration — Druid is a generic analytics engine, data sensitivity is a deployment concern
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Data-gateway archetype with read-only scope. Druid does not define data sensitivity — it stores whatever is ingested. Authorization scopes to datasource level, allowing agents to be restricted to non-sensitive datasources.

b1.reasoning: Read-only scope and data-gateway archetype — B1 applies to write-enabled scope

b2.reasoning: Datasource-level authorization provides differentiation. Agents can be restricted to specific datasources.

b3.reasoning: No formal classification metadata exists — but this is expected for a generic analytics engine where data classification is a deployment concern.

findings.title: Rich Temporal Metadata, No Freshness Headers
findings.description: Time-series data with __time column. No API response freshness headers (Cache-Control, X-Data-Age). Data-gateway archetype — INFO.
findings.gap: No freshness signal in API responses.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding response headers for data freshness.

findings.title: No Data Quality Metrics
findings.description: No formal data quality scoring or completeness metrics within Druid.
findings.gap: No data quality signals for agents.
findings.recommendation: Consider exposing metadata about ingestion completeness.

findings.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names
findings.description: Druid uses clear, semantically meaningful field names (dataSource, intervals, dimensions, aggregations, granularity). No legacy codes.
findings.gap: None.
findings.recommendation: No action needed.

findings.title: System Tables Provide Discovery
findings.description: INFORMATION_SCHEMA queryable via SQL. Metadata API endpoints provide datasource and column discovery.
findings.gap: No external data catalog integration.
findings.recommendation: Consider registering in a centralized catalog.

findings.title: Operational Metrics Only
findings.description: Druid emits query/time, query/bytes metrics. No business outcome metrics.
findings.gap: No agent-specific business metrics.
findings.recommendation: Define agent-specific business metrics.
